places.name: Casa Batlló
places.description: One of Antoni Gaudí's most classic buildings is well-known for its "dragon-back" design.

places.name: Sagrada Família
places.description: Construction of Barcelona's iconic (but controversial) church is expected to be completed in 2026—a century after the death of its architect.

places.name: Labyrinth Park of Horta
places.description: This semi-secret storybook hedge maze is also the oldest garden in the city.

places.name: Park Güell
places.description: Gaudí's famously flamboyant architectural style is on full display in this sprawling park and garden.

places.name: Calder Mercury Fountain
places.description: A beautiful but toxic fountain of mercury.

places.name: Montjuïc Cemetery
places.description: This cemetery features modernist statues as strange as its name.

places.name: Bunkers of Carmel
places.description: These secluded and abandoned anti-aircraft defenses have become one the most scenic viewpoints in Barcelona.

places.name: Chocolate Museum
places.description: Gaze upon chocolate sculptures depicting everything from chariot races to Gaudí's famous church at this shrine to sweets.

places.name: Caelum
places.description: This cafe offers a curated collection of treats and tipples made by monks and nuns.

places.name: Bar Marsella
places.description: A centuries-old bar once frequented by Picasso, Hemingway, and Dalí.
